title: boronia fastigiata subsp. fastigiata
intro: Boronia fastigiata subsp. fastigiata, also known as the tall boronia, is a flowering plant native to the eastern coast of Australia. It is a shrub with clusters of pink to purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. The flowers and leaves of the plant are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
